description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Welcome/onboarding screen for first-time users: full-screen page showing what OpenWork can do (spreadsheets, browser automation, files, tasks, content, APIs) with a single "Get started" CTA
- Folder explanation in the local workspace panel: users now understand that the folder they pick becomes their workspace and that OpenWork reads/writes files in it
Fixes & polish
- Fixed Lexical SyncPlugin crash that was taking down the entire React tree on 0.12.7 (black screen on launch). The $getRoot() call was outside an editor read context
- Per-server enable/disable toggle in workspace MCP settings (#1562)
Packaging / ops
- Updated AUR PKGBUILD for 0.12.7
- 7 new eval flows for onboarding (Flows 20-26)
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.12.7...v0.12.8
